name: Earlene Sharp's Carrot Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together the corn oil, granulated sugar, and eggs until well combined.
2. In a separate bowl, sift together the flour, baking soda, salt, and ground cinnamon.
3. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet mixture, stirring continuously.
4. Stir in the vanilla extract, ensuring it is well incorporated.
5. Mix in the grated carrots, chopped walnuts, flaked coconut, and dried pineapple until evenly distributed.
6. Grease and flour a 9- or 10-inch bundt pan.
7. Pour the batter into the prepared pan.
8. Bake at 350 degrees Fahrenheit for about an hour, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
9. Remove from the oven and let cool on a rack.
10. Prepare the cream cheese icing: Beat together two (8 oz) packages of cream cheese with 1-1/2 cups confectioners sugar and 1 teaspoon vanilla until smooth.
11. Spread the icing over the cooled cake.
12. Garnish with additional grated carrots and chopped nuts, if desired.
